tasks.title: [infra] Project scaffold — Tauri v2 + React + TanStack Router shell
tasks.description: Set up base project structure with Tauri v2 desktop+mobile targets, React 19 with TanStack Router, Tailwind v4 with Lunaris CSS custom properties for all 38 design variables (Light/Dark), font imports (JetBrains Mono, Geist). Verify dev server launches. Steps: 1) Configure tailwind.config.ts with all token map extensions from .plans/design-token-map.md 2) Add CSS custom properties in globals.css for Light/Dark themes 3) Import JetBrains Mono and Geist fonts 4) Configure TanStack Router with route stubs 5) Verify Tauri desktop shell launches with themed page.
tasks.testStrategy: Verify dev server starts, Tauri window opens, theme variables apply correctly in both Light and Dark modes.

tasks.title: [infra] Route configuration — desktop and mobile layouts
tasks.description: Configure TanStack Router with all routes from the PRD. Desktop routes use SidebarPageTemplate layout (sidebar + content). Draft flow routes (lobby, live, results) use TopBarPageTemplate. Mobile routes use MobileTabTemplate or MobileHeaderTemplate. Steps: 1) Create route tree with all desktop routes 2) Create route tree with all mobile routes 3) Add layout route wrappers for each template type 4) Add platform detection (desktop vs mobile) for layout selection.
tasks.testStrategy: Navigate to each route, verify correct layout wrapper renders.

tasks.title: [infra] Google OAuth integration — auth flow + token storage
tasks.description: Implement Google OAuth 2.0 sign-in with @uwo.ca domain restriction. Request spreadsheets.edit and profile scopes. Store OAuth tokens in OS keychain via Tauri. Steps: 1) Set up Google OAuth client config 2) Implement OAuth redirect flow with Tauri deep links 3) Add domain validation (@uwo.ca only) 4) Store refresh token in OS keychain via tauri-plugin-store 5) Create auth state provider with session persistence.
tasks.testStrategy: Test OAuth flow with mock Google responses, verify domain restriction, verify token persistence across app restarts.

tasks.title: [infra] Zustand store setup — auth, draft, schedule, trade slices
tasks.description: Create Zustand store with typed slices for each domain: auth (user, role, session), draft (state machine, board, participants), schedule (shifts, quotas), trades (proposals, status). Steps: 1) Create auth slice with role-based access 2) Create draft slice with state machine (Created/Lobby/Live/Paused/Completed/Cancelled) 3) Create schedule slice for shifts and quota tracking 4) Create trade slice with trade lifecycle states.
tasks.testStrategy: Unit test each slice with state transitions, verify type safety.

tasks.title: [infra] WebSocket client — tokio-tungstenite Tauri IPC bridge
tasks.description: Create WebSocket client infrastructure for real-time draft communication. Bridge between Rust WebSocket (tokio-tungstenite) and React via Tauri IPC events. Steps: 1) Create Rust WebSocket connection manager with auto-reconnect 2) Define typed message protocol (pick, reaction, state sync, timer) 3) Create Tauri IPC event bridge (ws_connect, ws_send, ws_on_message) 4) Create React hook useWebSocket with connection state management 5) Add reconnection UI overlay component.
tasks.testStrategy: Test connection lifecycle, message serialization, reconnection behavior with mock server.

tasks.title: [api] Sheet Adapter — workbook parser (structure detection)
tasks.description: Rust module that parses Google Sheets workbook structure. Detect variable-size week blocks by scanning column A for 'Week N' patterns. Calculate block size and sub-area count. Read data validation rules to map roster ranges to duty rows. Steps: 1) Implement Google Sheets API v4 client with OAuth token 2) Scan column A for week header rows, build week index 3) Calculate block size from consecutive week gaps, derive sub-area count 4) Read data validation rules to identify roster source ranges 5) Extract sub-area labels from column A formulas.
tasks.details: Block size = gap between consecutive 'Week N' rows. Sub-area count = block_size - 2. For Mt. Taylor: 3 sub-areas, block size 5.
tasks.testStrategy: Test against mock Sheet data matching Mt. Taylor workbook structure. Verify correct detection of 3 sub-areas, 17-18 weeks, roster ranges.

tasks.title: [api] Sheet Adapter — roster and quota reader
tasks.description: Read Gryphon rosters from identified ranges and Duty Math quota targets. Steps: 1) Read each roster range to extract Gryphon names per sub-area 2) Read Duty Math sheets to extract per-Gryphon weekday/weekend quotas 3) Read Holidays sheet to flag holiday dates 4) Build typed structs for roster, quotas, and holidays.
tasks.testStrategy: Verify roster extraction matches known Mt. Taylor data (TayMoja: 8, Houses: 8, Sayre: 10). Verify quota calculations.

tasks.title: [api] Sheet Adapter — pre-fill scanner and shift reader
tasks.description: Scan all duty rows for a given sub-area and semester to identify pre-filled shifts (non-empty cells). Build the draft board initial state. Steps: 1) Iterate week blocks for selected semester range 2) For each duty row in the sub-area, read columns B-J 3) Mark non-empty cells as pre-filled with assigned name 4) Build ShiftGrid struct with cell states (available, pre-filled) and calendar dates from date rows.
tasks.details: Date row is at week_header_row (offset +0), columns B-J contain datetime values mapping to calendar days.
tasks.testStrategy: Test with mock Sheet data containing mix of empty and pre-filled cells. Verify correct cell-to-date mapping.

tasks.title: [api] Sheet Adapter — write operations (pick, swap, clear)
tasks.description: Implement the three write operations using Google Sheets API batchUpdate with ValueInputOption.USER_ENTERED. Steps: 1) write_pick(sub_area_index, week_header_row, column, name) — writes name to duty cell 2) swap_trade(cell_a, cell_b) — reads both, writes each to other in single batch 3) clear_pick(cell) — clears a cell for undo/cancellation 4) Add rate limiter (300 writes/min) with queue.
tasks.details: duty_row = week_header_row + 2 + sub_area_index. Never write to quota columns (L+) — only B-J on duty rows.
tasks.testStrategy: Test write operations against mock Sheet API. Verify rate limiting. Verify formula columns are never touched.

tasks.title: [api] Sheet Adapter — write-through cache
tasks.description: Implement in-memory cache that is authoritative during live drafts. Queue async writes to Sheets. Steps: 1) Create in-memory ShiftGrid cache updated on every pick 2) Queue Sheets writes with async executor 3) Implement exponential backoff retry on write failure 4) Add post-draft reconciliation step that verifies all picks were written.
tasks.details: During live draft: in-memory state is authoritative, Sheets writes are async. After draft: reconciliation verifies consistency.
tasks.testStrategy: Test cache hit/miss behavior, retry logic, reconciliation detection of mismatches.

tasks.title: [api] Draft engine — state machine + turn management
tasks.description: Implement the draft lifecycle state machine (Created→Lobby→Live→Paused→Completed→Cancelled) and turn order logic. Steps: 1) Implement state machine with valid transitions and guards 2) Implement Snake mode (reverse order each round) 3) Implement Linear mode (same order) 4) Implement Random mode (re-shuffle each round) 5) Add per-pick countdown timer with server-authoritative timestamps.
tasks.details: State transitions: Created→Lobby, Lobby→Live/Cancelled, Live→Paused/Completed, Paused→Live/Cancelled. Only Head Gryphon can start/pause/resume/cancel.
tasks.testStrategy: Unit test all state transitions including invalid ones. Test turn order for each mode across multiple rounds.

tasks.title: [api] Draft engine — pick validation + broadcast
tasks.description: Validate and process draft picks, broadcast state updates via WebSocket. Steps: 1) Validate pick: correct turn, cell available, not pre-filled, timer not expired 2) Update in-memory draft state on valid pick 3) Broadcast cell update to all connected clients via WebSocket 4) Queue async Sheet write via write-through cache 5) Handle timer expiry (auto-pick/skip per config).
tasks.details: Pick latency target: <200ms from WebSocket receive to broadcast. Sheet write is async and non-blocking.
tasks.testStrategy: Test valid and invalid pick scenarios. Verify broadcast timing. Test timer expiry behavior.

tasks.title: [api] Trade engine — propose, accept, decline, expire
tasks.description: Implement trade lifecycle: propose (broadcast or direct), accept (swap Sheet cells), decline, expire. Steps: 1) Create trade proposal (public broadcast or direct to specific Gryphon) 2) Accept trade — call Sheet Adapter swap_trade to swap two cell values 3) Decline trade — update state, notify proposer 4) Expire trade — background timer, auto-expire unanswered proposals 5) Cancel trade — proposer can cancel before acceptance.
tasks.details: On accept: COUNTIFs auto-recalculate in Sheet. Trade only valid for uncompleted shifts.
tasks.testStrategy: Test full trade lifecycle. Verify Sheet cell swap on acceptance. Test expiry timer.

tasks.title: [api] Coordination server — Axum WebSocket host
tasks.description: Set up Axum server for WebSocket hosting, draft state management, and Sheet proxy. Steps: 1) Create Axum server with WebSocket upgrade handler 2) Implement room management (one room per draft) 3) Add RBAC middleware (Admin/Head Gryphon/Gryphon permissions) 4) Implement emoji reaction broadcast (ephemeral, fire-and-forget) 5) Add health check and connection monitoring endpoints.
tasks.details: Server holds authoritative draft state in-memory. WebSocket messages: pick_shift, send_reaction, draft_state_sync, timer_sync.
tasks.testStrategy: Test WebSocket connection lifecycle, room join/leave, RBAC enforcement, concurrent connections.

tasks.title: [api] SQLite local cache — offline schedule viewing
tasks.description: Set up rusqlite for local caching of schedule data for offline viewing. Steps: 1) Create SQLite schema for cached rosters, shifts, and quotas 2) Sync from Sheet data on last connection 3) Create Tauri IPC commands for reading cached data 4) Add last-synced timestamp and stale data indicator.
tasks.details: Read-only cache. Live draft requires active connection. Offline trade proposals queued for later submission.
tasks.testStrategy: Test cache population, offline read, stale detection.

tasks.title: [view] Button atom — all variants
tasks.description: Build Button component matching Lunaris design system. Variants: Default (primary), Secondary, Destructive, Outline, Ghost — each in regular (h-40) and large (h-48) sizes. All use rounded-full (--radius-pill). Ref: Pencil IDs ZETEA, U83R7, ftEoU, 4x7RU, Svd9t + large variants. Steps: 1) Create Button component with variant and size props 2) Apply Lunaris tokens (fill colors, text, shadow, border) 3) Add icon slot support (left/right icon) 4) Add loading/disabled states.
tasks.details: Font: font-secondary (Geist), weight 500, size 14px. Padding: regular 10px 16px, large 12px 24px. All corners rounded-full.
tasks.testStrategy: Render test for each variant x size combination. Verify disabled and loading states.

tasks.title: [view] IconButton atom — all variants
tasks.description: Build IconButton component. Same variants as Button but square (icon-only, no text). Regular: 40x40, Large: 48x48. Ref: Pencil IDs pEY1B, HWbHA, ai6BP, xQWwZ, eNU2w + large variants. Steps: 1) Create IconButton extending Button with icon-only layout 2) Apply same variant styles as Button 3) Add Material Symbols icon support.
tasks.details: Uses Material Symbols Outlined/Rounded icon font. Default icon size 24px.
tasks.testStrategy: Render test for each variant. Verify icon renders correctly.

tasks.title: [view] Input atom — text input with label and states
tasks.description: Build Input component with label group. States: default (placeholder), filled (value), error, disabled. Ref: Pencil IDs gKpi4, z6HCm, 592AB, URXVp. Steps: 1) Create Input with label, placeholder, and value states 2) Apply Lunaris tokens (border --input, background, text colors) 3) Add error state with message 4) Add disabled state styling.
tasks.details: Input group gap: 6px. Input padding: ~10px 12px. Font: font-secondary 14px. Label: font-secondary font-medium 14px.
tasks.testStrategy: Render test for each state. Test controlled/uncontrolled input behavior.

tasks.title: [view] Select, Textarea, SearchBox atoms
tasks.description: Build Select (dropdown trigger), Textarea (multi-line), and SearchBox (search input with icon) components. Ref: Pencil IDs XhJWF, QFzE8, T5yK2/Zksub. Steps: 1) Create Select with default/filled states and chevron icon 2) Create Textarea with default/filled states 3) Create SearchBox with search icon, default/filled states 4) Apply consistent Lunaris input styling across all three.
tasks.details: Select group gap 6px. Textarea has resize handle. SearchBox has search icon left, close icon when filled (240px default width).
tasks.testStrategy: Render tests for each component in each state.

tasks.title: [view] Avatar atom — text and image variants
tasks.description: Build Avatar component. Text variant: initials on --secondary background. Image variant: circular image. Size: 40x40, rounded-full. Ref: Pencil IDs 90SQo, 4AN1p. Steps: 1) Create Avatar with text (initials) variant 2) Add image variant with fallback to text 3) Apply Lunaris tokens (secondary bg, foreground text).
tasks.details: 40x40px, rounded-full, font-secondary for initials.
tasks.testStrategy: Test text and image variants, image fallback behavior.

tasks.title: [view] Label/Badge atom — status variants
tasks.description: Build Label/Badge component. Variants: Secondary, Success, Violet/Info, Orange/Warning — each with optional icon. Ref: Pencil IDs it00G, 7KC5U, rjvI1, L8Rgv + icon variants. Steps: 1) Create Label with variant prop controlling colors 2) Add optional leading icon slot 3) Apply semantic color tokens per variant 4) Use rounded-full, padding 8px.
tasks.details: Success uses --color-success bg + --color-success-foreground text. Orange uses --color-warning tokens. Violet uses --color-info tokens.
tasks.testStrategy: Render each variant with and without icon.

tasks.title: [view] Alert atom — info, success, warning, error
tasks.description: Build Alert component with four semantic variants. Each has icon, title, and description. Ref: Pencil IDs ITZkn, nIj3a, vbyqV, YZjRF. Steps: 1) Create Alert with variant prop 2) Add icon, title, description slots 3) Apply semantic color tokens (bg + foreground per variant) 4) Use --radius-none corners, padding 16px, gap 12px.
tasks.details: Alert/Info is the base component. Others are ref variants with different colors and icons.
tasks.testStrategy: Render each variant. Verify correct colors apply for each.

tasks.title: [view] Checkbox, Radio, Switch atoms
tasks.description: Build form control atoms: Checkbox (default/checked, with/without description), Radio (default/selected, with/without description), Switch (default/checked with label). Ref: Pencil IDs Wxq1C, r91nP, VYDf7, Ao9E1, u61z6, yoahP, wk1O8, zdFKu. Steps: 1) Create Checkbox with checked state + optional description 2) Create Radio with selected state + optional description 3) Create Switch with checked state + label 4) Apply Lunaris tokens and accessible ARIA roles.
tasks.details: Checkbox/Radio: 16x16px. Switch: 32x20px track. All use --foreground for checked state.
tasks.testStrategy: Test toggle behavior, keyboard interaction, ARIA attributes.

tasks.title: [view] Progress, Tooltip, BreadcrumbItem atoms
tasks.description: Build remaining atoms: Progress bar (--primary on --secondary track), Tooltip (popover with text), BreadcrumbItem (default, active, separator, ellipsis). Ref: Pencil IDs W4YFH, xCEfn, nW26m/mErlM/axCNF/xzG1l. Steps: 1) Create Progress with value prop (0-100) 2) Create Tooltip with text and positioning 3) Create BreadcrumbItem with four variants 4) Apply Lunaris tokens.
tasks.details: Progress: 16px height, rounded-full, fill_container width. Tooltip: rounded-full, shadow, --popover bg.
tasks.testStrategy: Test Progress at various values. Test Tooltip positioning. Test Breadcrumb variant rendering.

tasks.title: [view] Tabs molecule — active/inactive tab switching
tasks.description: Build Tabs molecule composing TabItem atoms. Container with --secondary bg, rounded-full, active item gets --background bg with shadow. Ref: Pencil IDs Kbr4h, KbyBJ, BdBJJ. Steps: 1) Create TabItem with active/inactive states 2) Create Tabs container with controlled active tab 3) Apply Lunaris tokens (secondary bg, shadow on active).
tasks.details: Container padding 4px, gap 8px, rounded-full. Active tab: shadow + --background fill. Inactive: --muted-foreground text.
tasks.testStrategy: Test tab switching, verify active state styling.

tasks.title: [view] Pagination molecule
tasks.description: Build Pagination molecule composing PaginationItem atoms. Shows page numbers, prev/next, ellipsis. Ref: Pencil IDs 9PVw5, oT0d2, Doslm, Irk3I. Steps: 1) Create PaginationItem (active, default, ellipsis) 2) Create Pagination with page count, current page, onChange 3) Add prev/next navigation arrows.
tasks.details: Items: 40x40px, rounded-full. Active: shadow + border + --background fill. Default: no fill.
tasks.testStrategy: Test pagination with various page counts, boundary behavior.

tasks.title: [view] Sidebar organism — navigation shell
tasks.description: Build Sidebar organism with logo, section titles, nav items (active/default), and user info at bottom. Width: 280px. Ref: Pencil IDs d5ZTS, h12kK, dOLzc, X6nwq. Steps: 1) Create SidebarSectionTitle component 2) Create SidebarItem with active/default states and icon 3) Compose Sidebar with logo, nav sections, user avatar/name/email 4) Apply --sidebar color tokens, border-right.
tasks.details: Width 280px, bg --sidebar, right border --sidebar-border. Active item: --sidebar-accent bg, rounded-full. Default: --sidebar-foreground text.
tasks.testStrategy: Test with different nav configurations. Verify active state updates on route change.

tasks.title: [view] Card organism — all variants
tasks.description: Build Card organism with header (title + subtitle), content slot, and footer (actions) slot. Variants: plain (stat card), action (with button), image (with image header). Ref: Pencil IDs ERkuB, eBwLd, wg5F3, ksvfk. Steps: 1) Create base Card with header/content/footer slots 2) Create CardPlain variant (stat number + label) 3) Create CardAction variant (content + action button) 4) Create CardImage variant (image header + content + actions) 5) Apply --card bg, --border stroke, --radius-none corners.
tasks.details: Card padding: 24px per section. Gap: 24px between sections. Border: 1px --border. No corner radius (--radius-none).
tasks.testStrategy: Render each variant with realistic content. Verify slot composition.

tasks.title: [view] DataTable organism — header, table, footer
tasks.description: Build DataTable organism composing: DataTableHeader (search + actions), Table (rows with cells), DataTableFooter (pagination + row count). Ref: Pencil IDs yLiVX, VnGc5, pPOgy, T73Cd, uKYIj, tbrR4, j9N2Y. Steps: 1) Create TableColumnHeader component 2) Create TableCell component 3) Create TableRow composing cells 4) Create Table with header row + data rows 5) Compose DataTable with DataTableHeader + Table + DataTableFooter.
tasks.details: Table follows strict hierarchy: Table→TableRow→TableCell→content. Cell padding 12px. Row border-bottom 1px --border.
tasks.testStrategy: Test with varying row counts, column configurations. Test search filtering, pagination integration.

tasks.title: [view] Dialog and Modal organisms
tasks.description: Build Dialog (confirmation) and Modal (left, center, center-icon) organisms extending Card. Ref: Pencil IDs cYAuh, izigX, 5JUG0, DBtsv. Steps: 1) Create Dialog with title, description, confirm/cancel buttons 2) Create Modal/Left with left-aligned title + actions 3) Create Modal/Center with centered title + actions 4) Create Modal/CenterIcon with icon + centered content 5) Add overlay backdrop and close-on-escape.
tasks.details: Dialog: 480px width, --background fill. Modals: 400px width. All use Card as base with customized sections.
tasks.testStrategy: Test open/close behavior, focus trapping, keyboard escape, overlay click.

tasks.title: [view] Accordion and Dropdown molecules
tasks.description: Build Accordion (open/closed with expand/collapse) and Dropdown (popover list with search, items, dividers). Ref: Pencil IDs 3bQzF, DFcCn, cH4wO. Steps: 1) Create Accordion with open/closed states and chevron toggle 2) Create Dropdown with slot for ListItems, SearchBox, Dividers 3) Add Dropdown positioning and outside-click-to-close.
tasks.details: Accordion: border-bottom --border. Dropdown: 240px width, --popover bg, --radius-m corners, shadow.
tasks.testStrategy: Test expand/collapse animation, dropdown positioning, keyboard navigation.

tasks.title: [view] MobileTabBar organism — bottom navigation
tasks.description: Build bottom tab bar for mobile screens with 4 tabs: Home, My Shifts, Draft, Schedule. Ref: Mobile screens in mockup. Steps: 1) Create TabBarItem with icon + label, active/inactive states 2) Create MobileTabBar with 4 fixed tabs 3) Integrate with TanStack Router for active tab detection 4) Apply Lunaris tokens (--card bg, --border top, 72px height).
tasks.details: Height 72px, padding 8px 0 20px 0. Tab items evenly distributed (justifyContent space_around).
tasks.testStrategy: Test tab switching, active state, route integration.

tasks.title: [view] DraftBoard feature component — shift grid
tasks.description: Build the draft board grid: 9 columns (Sun Day–Sat Night) × N weeks. Cell states: available, draft-picked (avatar+name), pre-filled (lock icon, muted), selected (highlighted). Steps: 1) Create DraftCell with 4 states (available, picked, pre-filled, selected) 2) Create column headers matching Sheet layout (B-J) 3) Create week row grouping with week number labels 4) Compose grid with horizontal scroll on mobile 5) Add holiday indicator on flagged cells.
tasks.details: Maps directly to Area Name sheet grid. Weekday=Sun Night–Wed (cols C-F), Weekend=Sun Day+Thu–Sat Night (cols B,G-J). Pre-filled cells show lock icon + muted bg.
tasks.testStrategy: Render grid with mock data including all cell states. Test cell selection on available cells. Verify pre-filled cells are not interactive.

tasks.title: [view] DraftTopBar feature component — turn indicator, timer, progress
tasks.description: Build draft top bar with: turn indicator badge ('Round X · [Name]'s Turn'), countdown timer, progress bar ('X/Y shifts' excluding pre-fills), pause button (Head Gryphon only). Steps: 1) Create turn indicator with round/name display 2) Create countdown timer with server-synced time 3) Create progress bar showing drafted/total available 4) Add pause/resume button (role-gated).
tasks.details: Top bar height 64px, padding 0 32px, border-bottom. Timer accuracy target: 500ms across clients.
tasks.testStrategy: Test timer countdown, turn indicator updates, progress calculation excluding pre-fills.

tasks.title: [view] QuotaSidebar feature component — weekday/weekend progress
tasks.description: Build quota sidebar showing each Gryphon's weekday/weekend shift progress vs. Duty Math targets. Steps: 1) Create Gryphon quota row with avatar, name, weekday count, weekend count 2) Create progress indicators (X of Y format) 3) Compose sidebar list of all Gryphons in draft 4) Highlight near-quota and quota-met states.
tasks.details: Reads quota targets from Duty Math sheet. Updates in real-time on each pick (from in-memory cache, not Sheet).
tasks.testStrategy: Test with mock quota data. Verify counts update on pick events.

tasks.title: [view] ReactionBar feature component — emoji picker + floating animations
tasks.description: Build emoji reaction bar and floating animation system for live draft. Steps: 1) Create reaction bar with preset emojis (fire, laughing, skull, clap) + custom picker 2) Create floating emoji animation component (rise + fade) 3) Integrate with WebSocket broadcast for real-time reactions 4) Position as overlay (mobile: bottom floating bar, desktop: inline).
tasks.details: Mobile: cornerRadius 24, bg #1A1A1ECC with background_blur, height 48px, width 329px, positioned at bottom. Ephemeral fire-and-forget events.
tasks.testStrategy: Test emoji selection, animation rendering, WebSocket broadcast/receive.

tasks.title: [view] ShiftCard feature component — mobile shift display
tasks.description: Build mobile shift card showing: day/type, date, time range, Trade button, status badge. Steps: 1) Create ShiftCard with shift data props 2) Add Trade button (navigates to Trade Terminal) 3) Add status badge (Assigned/Completed/Upcoming) 4) Apply Lunaris card styling.
tasks.details: Example: 'Monday Evening · Mon, Mar 3 · 6:00 PM–11:00 PM' with Trade button and status badge.
tasks.testStrategy: Render with various shift states. Test Trade button navigation.

tasks.title: [view] TradeCard feature component — trade proposal display
tasks.description: Build trade card showing: poster avatar/name, time posted, shift details, accept/decline or propose swap actions. Steps: 1) Create TradeCard with proposal data props 2) Add poster info (avatar, name, timestamp) 3) Add shift details display 4) Add action buttons (Accept/Decline for direct, Propose Swap for board).
tasks.details: Used in Trade Center (desktop tabs) and Trade Board (mobile).
tasks.testStrategy: Render with various trade states. Test action button callbacks.

tasks.title: [section] LoginSection — OAuth flow UI
tasks.description: Build Login section: centered card with 'GRYPHON DRAFT' branding and 'Continue with Google' button. Handle OAuth redirect and error states. Covers US-001. Steps: 1) Create LoginCard with branding, subtitle, Google button 2) Wire Google OAuth flow from auth store 3) Add error state display (invalid domain, cancelled, failed) 4) Add redirect to role-appropriate dashboard on success.
tasks.details: Dark mode default. Card: --card bg, --border stroke, centered at ~510,200. Button: primary variant with Google icon.
tasks.testStrategy: Test OAuth flow with mocked responses. Verify domain restriction error. Verify role-based redirect.

tasks.title: [section] AdminUserManagementSection — user data table
tasks.description: Build Admin user management section with DataTable of Gryphons. Covers US-002. Steps: 1) Create user data table with columns: Name, Email, Role (badge), Residence/Sub-area, Actions 2) Add search filtering in DataTableHeader 3) Add 'Add User' button with modal form 4) Add 'Invite' button functionality 5) Wire data from Sheet roster via IPC.
tasks.details: Uses SidebarPageTemplate layout. Sidebar nav: Users (active), Invitations, Google Sheet, Settings.
tasks.testStrategy: Test table rendering with mock user data. Test search filtering. Test add user modal flow.

tasks.title: [section] HeadGryphonDashboardSection — schedule calendar + quotas
tasks.description: Build HG Dashboard with schedule overview calendar and quota sidebar. Covers US-003, US-004. Steps: 1) Create schedule calendar grid showing all weeks 2) Apply visual distinction for pre-filled vs draft-assigned vs unassigned cells 3) Create quota summary panel with per-Gryphon weekday/weekend counts 4) Add 'Create Draft' action button 5) Wire data from Sheet Adapter (rosters, shifts, quotas).
tasks.details: Uses SidebarPageTemplate. Sidebar nav: Schedule (active), Drafts, Trades, My Gryphons.
tasks.testStrategy: Test calendar render with mixed shift states. Verify quota display matches Sheet COUNTIF data.

tasks.title: [section] GryphonDashboardSection — stats + upcoming + draft alert
tasks.description: Build Gryphon Dashboard with greeting, stat cards, upcoming shifts, draft alert. Covers US-005. Steps: 1) Create greeting header ('Welcome back, [Name]') with role/residence subtitle 2) Create 4 stat cards (total, completed, upcoming, trades) using CardPlain pattern 3) Create upcoming shift card 4) Create draft alert notification with 'Join Draft' button 5) Create on-duty status card with live indicator.
tasks.details: Uses SidebarPageTemplate. Sidebar nav: Dashboard (active), Drafts, Trades, Profile.
tasks.testStrategy: Test with various states: shifts assigned, no shifts, draft available, currently on duty.

tasks.title: [section] DraftsListSection — draft cards grid + create draft flow
tasks.description: Build Drafts list with grid of draft cards and Create Draft form. Covers US-006. Steps: 1) Create draft card component showing draft status, sub-area, date 2) Create draft cards grid layout 3) Create 'Create Draft' form: workbook select, semester, sub-area, pre-fill summary 4) Create draft config: mode (Snake/Linear/Random), timer, draft order with shuffle 5) Wire Sheet scan for pre-fill count.
tasks.details: Uses SidebarPageTemplate. Pre-fill summary: 'X shifts already assigned, Y available for draft.'
tasks.testStrategy: Test draft card rendering. Test create draft form validation. Test pre-fill count display.

tasks.title: [section] DraftLobbySection — waiting room + participant list
tasks.description: Build Draft Lobby with config info, participant list, and start controls. Covers US-007. Steps: 1) Create lobby info card with draft config + pre-fill summary 2) Create participant list with ready/not-ready status indicators 3) Add 'Ready' button for participants 4) Add 'Start Draft' button (Head Gryphon only, gated by role) 5) Wire WebSocket for real-time participant updates.
tasks.details: Uses TopBarPageTemplate (no sidebar). Re-scans Sheet on transition to Live.
tasks.testStrategy: Test participant join/ready flow. Verify 'Start Draft' is role-gated. Test real-time updates.

tasks.title: [section] LiveDraftSection — draft board + reactions + quota sidebar
tasks.description: Build the core Live Draft experience composing DraftBoard, DraftTopBar, QuotaSidebar, and ReactionBar. Covers US-008, US-009, US-010, US-011, US-021, US-022, US-023. Steps: 1) Compose DraftTopBar + DraftBoard + QuotaSidebar layout 2) Wire pick flow: tap cell → validate → confirm → WebSocket broadcast 3) Wire turn management from draft engine state 4) Add ReactionBar overlay 5) Handle pause/resume/cancel (Head Gryphon controls).
tasks.details: Uses TopBarPageTemplate. Pick latency <200ms. Timer synced to server. Emoji reactions ephemeral.
tasks.testStrategy: Test full pick flow end-to-end with mocked WebSocket. Verify turn order for each draft mode. Test pause/resume.

tasks.title: [section] DraftResultsSection — post-draft summary + schedule table
tasks.description: Build Draft Results with personal stats and full schedule table. Covers US-012. Steps: 1) Create personal stats display (total picked, weekend, night shifts) 2) Create full schedule table with source column (Draft-Picked vs. Pre-Filled) 3) Add 'Go to Trade Center' action 4) Wire data from completed draft state.
tasks.details: Uses TopBarPageTemplate. Sheet is fully up-to-date at this point (reconciliation complete).
tasks.testStrategy: Test stats calculation from draft results. Verify source column accuracy.

tasks.title: [section] TradeCenterSection — trade board + tabs
tasks.description: Build Trade Center with Open/Completed tabs, trade cards, and propose swap flow. Covers US-013, US-014, US-015. Steps: 1) Create tabs for Open and Completed trades 2) Render trade cards with poster info, shift details, actions 3) Wire accept/decline actions to trade engine 4) Add 'Propose Swap' flow for open board trades 5) Wire Sheet Adapter swap_trade on acceptance.
tasks.details: Uses SidebarPageTemplate. Sidebar nav: Trades (active). On accept: COUNTIFs auto-recalculate.
tasks.testStrategy: Test trade card rendering. Test accept/decline flow. Verify Sheet swap call.

tasks.title: [section] ProfileSection — account settings
tasks.description: Build Profile section with avatar, name, email, role badge, residence, and notification preferences. Covers US-016. Steps: 1) Create profile header with avatar, name, email 2) Add role badge and residence display 3) Create notification preferences toggles 4) Add avatar upload/change functionality.
tasks.details: Uses SidebarPageTemplate. Sidebar nav: Profile (active).
tasks.testStrategy: Test profile data display. Test notification toggle state changes.

tasks.title: [section] MobileDashboardSection — mobile home screen
tasks.description: Build mobile dashboard with greeting, on-duty card, upcoming shifts, draft status. Covers US-017. Steps: 1) Create mobile header with greeting + notification bell 2) Create On Duty card with live indicator 3) Create upcoming shifts list (3 cards + View All) 4) Create draft status card with 'Enter Draft Lobby' button 5) Wire to MobileTabTemplate with bottom tab bar.
tasks.details: Uses MobileTabTemplate. Header 56px, content padding 16px 20px, tab bar 72px.
tasks.testStrategy: Test with various states: on-duty, draft available, no upcoming shifts.

tasks.title: [section] MobileMyShiftsSection — quota progress + shift list
tasks.description: Build mobile My Shifts screen with quota progress bars, stats row, and shift cards with trade buttons. Covers US-018. Steps: 1) Create quota progress bars (weekday/weekend) 2) Create stats row (Assigned, Completed, Remaining counts) 3) Create shift cards list with Trade button per card 4) Wire trade navigation to Trade Terminal.
tasks.details: Uses MobileTabTemplate. Progress bars use Progress atom with label overlay.
tasks.testStrategy: Test quota display, stats calculation, trade button navigation.

tasks.title: [section] MobileDraftFlowSection — commence, lobby, portal, grid
tasks.description: Build mobile draft flow: Commence Draft (HG config form), Draft Lobby (countdown + participants), Draft Portal (live picker + timer + reactions), Live Draft Grid (calendar). Covers US-019. Steps: 1) Create Commence Draft form (name, date, time, timer, draft order + shuffle) 2) Create mobile lobby with countdown timer and participant list 3) Create Draft Portal with current picker display, round/pick indicator, timer 4) Create mobile calendar grid (9-column with horizontal scroll) 5) Add floating reaction bar overlay.
tasks.details: Uses MobileHeaderTemplate (no tab bar during draft). Calendar grid needs horizontal scroll at 393px width.
tasks.testStrategy: Test each draft flow step on mobile viewport. Test horizontal scroll on grid.

tasks.title: [section] MobileTradeFlowSection — terminal + trade board
tasks.description: Build mobile trade flow: Trade Terminal Step 1 (select shift), Step 2 (choose method), Trade Board (open trades + proposals). Covers US-013, US-014, US-015 (mobile). Steps: 1) Create Step 1: radio-style shift selection from assigned shifts 2) Create Step 2: broadcast vs. direct trade method picker 3) Create Trade Board with open trade cards + Propose Swap 4) Wire accept/decline for direct proposals 5) Wire trade engine for submissions.
tasks.details: Uses MobileHeaderTemplate. Step 1 note: 'You can only trade shifts that haven't been completed yet.'
tasks.testStrategy: Test two-step trade flow. Test trade board rendering. Test accept/decline on proposals.

tasks.title: [infra] Push notifications — draft starts + trade proposals
tasks.description: Implement push notification system for mobile. Covers US-020. Steps: 1) Set up push notification provider (FCM for Android, APNs for iOS) 2) Register device tokens on login 3) Send notifications on draft lobby creation and turn changes 4) Send notifications on incoming trade proposals 5) Add notification preferences (enable/disable per category).
tasks.details: Tauri v2 mobile supports push via native plugins. Notifications when app backgrounded.
tasks.testStrategy: Test notification registration, delivery simulation, preference toggling.

tasks.title: [e2e] Page objects for all screens
tasks.description: Create page object classes for all 20 screens to use in e2e tests. Steps: 1) Create LoginPage page object (Google button, error display) 2) Create DashboardPage objects (desktop + mobile variants) 3) Create DraftPage objects (lobby, live board, results) 4) Create TradePage objects (center, terminal steps, board) 5) Create ProfilePage page object.
tasks.details: Page objects encapsulate selectors and common interactions. Used by all e2e test specs.
tasks.testStrategy: Verify page objects can locate all critical elements on each screen.

tasks.title: [e2e] Auth + onboarding stories (US-001, US-003)
tasks.description: E2E tests for login flow and Sheet import. Covers: US-001 (Google OAuth login), US-003 (import workbook). Steps: 1) Test successful OAuth login with @uwo.ca account 2) Test OAuth rejection for non-@uwo.ca 3) Test OAuth cancellation/error display 4) Test Sheet import with valid workbook 5) Test Sheet import with invalid format.
tasks.details: Mock Google OAuth and Sheets API responses for deterministic tests.
tasks.testStrategy: Given/When/Then from ACs: AC-001.1 through AC-001.5, AC-003.1 through AC-003.5.

tasks.title: [e2e] Dashboard stories (US-002, US-004, US-005, US-017, US-018)
tasks.description: E2E tests for admin user management, HG dashboard, Gryphon dashboard, and mobile views. Steps: 1) Test admin user table search, add, invite 2) Test HG schedule calendar with quota display 3) Test Gryphon dashboard stats, upcoming shifts, draft alert 4) Test mobile dashboard with on-duty, upcoming, draft status 5) Test mobile My Shifts quota progress and trade button.
tasks.details: Test both desktop and mobile viewport sizes.
tasks.testStrategy: Given/When/Then from ACs across US-002, US-004, US-005, US-017, US-018.

tasks.title: [e2e] Draft flow stories (US-006–US-012, US-019, US-021–US-023)
tasks.description: E2E tests for the full draft lifecycle: create, lobby, live, results. Steps: 1) Test create draft with config selection and pre-fill summary 2) Test lobby join, ready, and start flow 3) Test live draft pick flow (tap → confirm → broadcast) 4) Test draft results display with stats and source column 5) Test mobile draft flow (portal, grid, reactions).
tasks.details: Requires WebSocket mock for real-time events. Test all 3 draft modes (Snake, Linear, Random).
tasks.testStrategy: Given/When/Then from ACs across US-006 through US-012, US-019, US-021–US-023.

tasks.title: [e2e] Trade flow stories (US-013–US-015)
tasks.description: E2E tests for trade lifecycle: propose, accept, decline, browse board. Steps: 1) Test propose trade (broadcast and direct methods) 2) Test accept trade (verify Sheet swap call) 3) Test decline trade 4) Test trade board browsing and propose swap 5) Test mobile trade terminal two-step flow.
tasks.details: Verify Sheet Adapter swap_trade is called on acceptance.
tasks.testStrategy: Given/When/Then from ACs across US-013, US-014, US-015.
